coverage.source: committed coverage/coverage-final.json (two exercised files only; static read, not --with-run)

checks.evidence: Jest only; no Vitest. Config at jest.config.ts.
RTL present in a React 19 project that ships tests.
user-event installed and used in the suite.
jest-dom installed.
Loaded via setupFilesAfterEnv (jest.config.ts:12); toHaveTextContent matchers used in the suite.
Explicit 80% thresholds across all four metrics.
Project lints with Biome, not ESLint; neither eslint-plugin-testing-library nor eslint-plugin-jest-dom is present.
checks.gap: Automated selector/async/matcher rules (no-node-access, prefer-screen-queries, prefer-find-by, no-wait-for-multiple-assertions) are unguarded by any linter. The current single test is clean, so the risk is latent for the next author.
checks.remediation: Adopt Biome equivalents where they exist or add a thin ESLint pass scoped to *.test.{ts,tsx} enabling the two plugins.

checks.evidence: Playwright present; config and behaviour spec exist.
8/12 (66.7%) Priority 1; 4/12 (33.3%) getByTestId.
checks.gap: 66.7% is 3.3 points below the 70% threshold, entirely due to four getByTestId('count') calls. Tiny denominator (12).
checks.remediation: The count renders visible text (ThresholdCounter.tsx:17-20); switch to an accessible query to reach 100% Priority 1.

checks.expectation: At most 10% of all queries use getByTestId.
checks.evidence: 4 of 12 queries (33.3%) use getByTestId('count') — over 3x the ceiling.
checks.gap:
data-testid='count' (ThresholdCounter.tsx:17) is a non-accessible hook; the underlying

carries real visible text. Every count assertion bypasses the accessibility tree.
checks.remediation: Give the count an accessible name (role=status/labelled region, or assert via getByText/getByRole) and delete the data-testid. One-file change plus four test lines.

checks.evidence: Zero container/document.querySelector calls.
All 12 queries are screen.getBy*; render return never destructured.
Render return never captured; no wrapper antipattern.
8/8 (100%) of Priority 1 queries are getByRole with a name option.
getByRole('status') resolves the implicit live-region role of <output> (ThresholdCounter.tsx:25); no redundant [role=...] assertions.
6 userEvent calls, 0 fireEvent -> 100% (threshold 80%).
Synchronous updates; no waitFor(() => getBy*()) misuse; findBy not needed.
No queryBy* usage.
No waitFor in the suite; none of the single-assertion / empty-callback / side-effect misuse patterns can occur.
Zero hand-rolled act() wrappings.
No cleanup import or call; Jest auto-cleanup relied upon.
Every query feeds an explicit expect(...); no bare-query assertion lines.

samples.match: it('warns as it approaches the limit, then reports capacity at the threshold')
it('never decrements below zero and can be reset')

checks.evidence: All names describe user-observable outcomes; none implementation-named. Promoted from soft partial to present.
Zero toHaveClass assertions; E2E skill explicitly forbids CSS-class assertions (run-e2e-tests/SKILL.md:27-30).
Zero snapshot tests; suite avoids the snapshot smell entirely.
80% thresholds set, but collectCoverageFrom (jest.config.ts:27-33) excludes App.tsx/main.tsx/index.ts; committed coverage covers only 2 files.
checks.gap: An 80% gate over a two-file surface is easy to keep green and misleads as the app grows. noGraphify means no centrality ranking of untested units.
checks.remediation: Re-include App.tsx (or cover it via E2E and document the exclusion), and revisit the exclude list per new top-level surface.

checks.evidence: coverageThreshold fails jest --coverage on regression, but pnpm verify runs plain jest without --coverage; no CI workflow found in this checkout.
checks.gap: The repo's own composite gate (verify) would not fail on a coverage drop; enforcement depends on an unobserved CI step.
checks.remediation: Point verify at test:coverage or add an explicit coverage CI job (one-line package.json:25 change).

samples.match: walks from healthy through warning to at-capacity, then resets

checks.evidence: One full role-driven user journey. styleproof.spec.ts is a computed-style capture (inert without STYLEMAP_DIR), not behavioural coverage.
Zero fixed waits; per-test render + userEvent.setup(); no shared mutable state; Playwright fullyParallel.
No mocks; component tested as a real unit. No async/error paths exist to mock.

samples.match: statusForCount: pure branchy rule, no direct unit test (only exercised through the component)
useThresholdCounter: no renderHook isolation test; reset-to-nonzero-initial path never exercised
App: no test, excluded from coverage

checks.evidence: Per-folder heuristic (noGraphify). The single feature component is tested, but the suite's surface is one happy path plus edges.
checks.gap: statusForCount (designed for isolation testing per its own docstring) has no direct test; the hook has no renderHook test; no error/async-path tests; no jest-axe a11y scan anywhere; App.tsx untested and excluded. The template sets no pattern beyond one synchronous feature.
checks.remediation: (a) Add a statusForCount table test (boundaries 0/3/4/7/8/9). (b) Add a renderHook test for useThresholdCounter covering reset to a non-zero initial. (c) Add jest-axe + one toHaveNoViolations assertion to lock in a11y.
